what_it_does: Records metadata for an image attachment linked to the design-source channel, pointing to the asset image://design-source/capital-raise/p-10.
observations: YAML front matter contains id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added timestamp
target uses a custom image://design-source/capital-raise/p-10 URI
No body content beyond the front matter
standouts: Custom image:// URI scheme for asset referencing
Attachment file acts as a ledger entry binding a channel to an external image asset
conventions: Markdown file used as a structured record container
Attachment id uses an 'att-' prefix with a UUID-style suffix
concerns: Target URI is opaque; no embedded checksum, size, or format metadata
No provenance or author field beyond the timestamp
notes: This is a lightweight channel attachment record that maps a design-source channel to a specific image asset.
questions: What does the image:// URI resolve to in storage?
Is 'p-10' a page, plate, or slide identifier?
Who added this attachment and for what purpose?
purpose_vs_actual: It records an image attachment reference, which matches its apparent purpose; n/a for a deeper mismatch.
intention_signals: To create a discoverable, timestamped ledger entry associating a design-source channel with an image asset for later retrieval or context.
novelty: Use of a channel-memory attachment file as a first-class reference object with a custom image:// URI.
contribution: A structured, timestamped attachment record that preserves the link between a design-source channel and a specific image asset.
summary_for_embedding: A channel-memory attachment metadata file in YAML front matter that registers an image asset at image://design-source/capital-raise/p-10 for the design-source channel.
intention_for_embedding: To catalog and make retrievable an image reference within the design-source channel's memory ledger.
